https://compshop.designflow.app/china 'Asia Trips' - 'Fair Trips' has a visual problem that no other section has. as you scroll images disappear and reappear. the scrolling is still not smooth. still looks like it's trying to reload the images, but they do appear a little faster. maybe it's not a caching/loading problem but a rendering problem? The images load and then when the page scroll down and then you scroll back up again it still takes 1.5-2 seconds to load the images instead of immediate / imperceptible beca8use they've already been loaded. Fix it

message.content:
<system-reminder>Message sent at Fri 2026-06-12 18:45:41 UTC.</system-reminder>
https://compshop.designflow.app/china 'Asia Trips' - 'Fair Trips' has a visual problem that no other section has. as you scroll images disappear and reappear. the scrolling is still not smooth. still looks like it's trying to reload the images, but they do appear a little faster. maybe it's not a caching/loading problem but a rendering problem? The images load and then when the page scroll down and then you scroll back up again it still takes 1.5-2 seconds to load the images instead of immediate / imperceptible beca8use they've already been loaded. Fix it

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Supabase
Here are guidelines for using Supabase tools effectively:

- Before making schema changes, use `list_tables` to understand the existing structure
- When debugging issues, start with `get_logs` and `get_advisors` before making changes
- Use `get_project_url` and `get_publishable_api_key` when helping users configure client-side integrations

If you have access to a local development environment with a filesystem and shell:
- Install the Supabase agent skill for critical development and security guidance: `npx skills add supabase/agent-skills` (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/ai-skills.md)
- Use the Supabase CLI (`supabase`) for local development workflows such as starting a local stack, managing migrations, and running edge functions locally (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/local-development.md)
- Prefer local development and testing before applying changes to a remote project

If you are running in a web-only or remote environment without filesystem or shell access:
- Rely on the MCP tools directly for all Supabase interactions
- Use `apply_migration` carefully, as changes go directly to the remote project

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Found the issue. The problem is on line 186 — images beyond the first 36 use `loading="lazy"` combined with `decoding="async"`. The browser treats lazy images as evictable from the render cache when they scroll off-screen, so scrolling back re-triggers decode even though the data is already fetched. Since this is a detail page users navigate to intentionally, all images should be eager.

content.content:
# ============================================================================
# Multi-stage build: Vite app → static files → Nginx
# Coolify will build this automatically when you point it at the repo.
# ============================================================================

# ---- Stage 1: build ----
FROM node:20-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app

# Build-time env vars must be passed as Docker build args by Coolify.
# Set these in Coolify → Application → Build Variables (NOT runtime env).
ARG VITE_SUPABASE_URL
ARG VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
ARG VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID

ENV VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$VITE_SUPABASE_URL
ENV VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=$VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
ENV VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID=$VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID

COPY package.json bun.lockb* package-lock.json* ./
RUN if [ -f bun.lockb ]; then \
      npm i -g bun && bun install; \
    else \
      npm ci; \
    fi

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# ---- Stage 2: serve ----
FROM nginx:1.27-alpine
COPY selfhost/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

EXPOSE 80
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

# ============================================================================
# Multi-stage build: Vite app → static files → Nginx
# Coolify will build this automatically when you point it at the repo.
# ============================================================================

# ---- Stage 1: build ----
FROM node:20-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app

# Build-time env vars must be passed as Docker build args by Coolify.
# Set these in Coolify → Application → Build Variables (NOT runtime env).
ARG VITE_SUPABASE_URL
ARG VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
ARG VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID

ENV VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$VITE_SUPABASE_URL
ENV VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=$VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
ENV VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID=$VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID

COPY package.json bun.lockb* package-lock.json* ./
RUN if [ -f bun.lockb ]; then \
      npm i -g bun && bun install; \
    else \
      npm ci; \
    fi

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# ---- Stage 2: serve ----
FROM nginx:1.27-alpine
COPY selfhost/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

EXPOSE 80
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

sudo git -C /home/ai/compshop add src/pages/FairTripStream.tsx && sudo git -C /home/ai/compshop commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix Fair Trips image disappear/reappear on scroll

Remove lazy loading and async decoding from Fair Trip stream images.
loading="lazy" allows browsers to evict images from the render cache when
they scroll offscreen, forcing re-decode on scroll-back even when the data
is already fetched. switching to eager+auto keeps images in the render
pipeline so re-entry into the viewport is imperceptible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

toolUseResult.stdout:
To https://github.com/u2giants/compshop.git

content.text:
Pushed. The fix removes two things:

1. **`loading="lazy"`** — was telling the browser it could evict off-screen images from the render cache. When you scrolled back up, the browser had to re-decode them from scratch even though the network data was still cached, causing the 1.5–2s delay.

2. **`decoding="async"`** — was letting the browser defer image decoding to a background thread, which added an extra repaint step on re-entry into the viewport.

Both images in the trip stream (video thumbnails and regular photos) are now `loading="eager"` + `decoding="auto"`. Since this is a detail page with pre-fetched signed URLs already in React state, eager loading is the right tradeoff — images stay in the render pipeline and reappear instantly on scroll-back. Coolify will rebuild and deploy shortly.

content: Fixed the Fair Trips scroll flicker by switching all images from lazy to eager loading and deploying. Waiting for Coolify to finish rebuilding so you can verify the fix at compshop.designflow.app/china. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text: My change made it worse — switching everything to `loading="eager"` floods the browser's HTTP connection pool with all images at once, causing them to queue for 5–10 seconds. Let me revert to the original priority split but keep `decoding="auto"` (that's the fix that actually addresses the scroll-back delay).

input.command:
sudo git -C /home/ai/compshop add src/pages/FairTripStream.tsx && sudo git -C /home/ai/compshop commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Revert eager-all loading; restore priority split, keep decoding=auto

All-eager loading saturated the HTTP connection pool causing 5-10s delays.
Restore the original eager-for-first-36 / lazy-for-rest split but keep
decoding="auto" (replacing "async") — that is the actual fix for the
1.5-2s scroll-back delay, since async decoding defers render even for
already-fetched images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && sudo git -C /home/ai/compshop push

content.content:
[main 9df1c16] Revert eager-all loading; restore priority split, keep decoding=auto

toolUseResult.stdout:
[main 9df1c16] Revert eager-all loading; restore priority split, keep decoding=auto
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
To https://github.com/u2giants/compshop.git
   9f7ebc9..9df1c16  main -> main

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what happened and what this fixes:

**What went wrong with my last change:** Making all images `loading="eager"` fires every request simultaneously. Browsers allow ~6 concurrent HTTP connections per origin, so with dozens or hundreds of images, most queued for 5–10 seconds.

**What this change does:**
- Restores the first-36-eager / rest-lazy split (so initial view loads fast, and below-the-fold images don't flood the connection pool)
- Keeps `decoding="auto"` instead of `decoding="async"` — this is the actual fix for the original problem. `decoding="async"` defers render to a background thread even for images that are already fetched, causing the blank-then-appears effect on scroll-back. `decoding="auto"` lets the browser decide, and it will sync-decode images that are already in cache, making scroll-back imperceptible.

content: Fixing Fair Trips image scroll rendering. A new build is deploying — test scrolling up and down on the Fair Trips page once it's live to confirm images stay visible. (disable recaps in /config)
